svg.text: Match rows by strain_id; each matching pair becomes one output row.
Three strains × five assays → five result rows. Strain 1 contributes twice (matches 101 and 102); strain 3 contributes twice (104, 105); strain 2 once.
Each output row is the concatenation of one strain row with one matching assay row. The strain_id column appears once because USING (strain_id) merges it.
Contrast: a strain with zero assays
Imagine a fourth strain (id 4, "JM109") in the strain table with no rows in assay.
INNER JOIN drops it from the result entirely — no matching assay row, no output row.
LEFT JOIN keeps it once, with NULL in every assay column (assay_id, medium, od600_24h).
